name: CQC — State of Care 2024/25
description: 45% of homes rated RI or Inadequate
statistic: 45% of care homes assessed under CQC's new single assessment framework and published in 2025 were rated Requires Improvement or Inadequate. This compares to a historical baseline of approximately 20% of all care homes rated RI or Inadequate across the full portfolio. Important caveat: CQC's assessment activity during 2024/25 has been risk-based — they prioritised inspecting services where data indicated greater risk to quality and safety. This means the 45% figure is not representative of all care homes in England but reflects outcomes among higher-risk services. The 3,062 adult social care ratings published under the single assessment framework (as at 1 August 2025) cover only a fraction of the 20,467 services with current ratings. The CQC itself warns that ratings under the new framework should not be directly compared with those from previous frameworks. Separately, the report notes that waits for care home beds and home-based care accounted for 45% of delayed hospital discharges for people in acute hospitals for 14+ days.
date: 24 October 2025 (covering 2024/25 period, ratings data as at 1 August 2025)
credibility: Government regulator. The CQC is the independent regulator of health and adult social care in England. The State of Care report is their flagship annual publication, laid before Parliament. It is the most authoritative source on care quality in England.
applicability: Directly care sector — specifically adult social care services in England, including residential care homes, nursing homes, domiciliary care, and supported living.
citable: Yes, with important caveats. The 45% figure can be cited but must include context about the risk-based assessment methodology. Recommended citation: 'CQC, The state of health care and adult social care in England 2024/25, October 2025.' When using on the website, include qualifying language such as 'Among care homes assessed under CQC's new framework in 2025, 45% were rated Requires Improvement or Inadequate — reflecting the regulator's focus on higher-risk services.'
website_placement: Pain point section — this statistic creates urgency around compliance and care quality. Can be used alongside the Clarica proposition: 'If nearly half of newly inspected care homes are falling short, the question is: how confident are you in your next inspection?' Works well in a data visualisation block showing the gap between aspiration (Outstanding/Good) and reality (RI/Inadequate). Also effective in CQC-focused content sections, blog posts about inspection readiness, and the social proof bar with appropriate context.
emotional_vs_rational: Primarily emotional — the idea that nearly half of inspected care homes are not meeting standards triggers concern, fear, and urgency among care home owners and managers. It speaks to professional pride, resident safety, and regulatory risk. The rational dimension comes from the financial and operational consequences of poor ratings: lost referrals, restricted admissions, increased regulatory scrutiny, and reputational damage.
clarica_differentiator: Strong differentiator with nuanced positioning. Clarica can use this stat to highlight the compliance gap that AI can help close: 'With 45% of newly inspected homes rated RI or Inadequate, maintaining consistent care quality and documentation is harder than ever. AI-powered compliance tools help you identify gaps before the regulator does.' This positions Clarica as a proactive compliance partner rather than a reactive software fix. The stat also differentiates Clarica from generic software vendors by framing the problem in regulatory terms that care managers understand and fear.
refresh_frequency: Annual. The CQC State of Care report is published every October. The next update (covering 2025/26) is expected in October 2026. CQC also publishes monthly ratings data updates through its online directory and data downloads.
comparison_pair: The key comparison is 45% (newly assessed under single assessment framework) vs. 20% (historical all-portfolio baseline). This dramatic difference highlights either deteriorating quality or the impact of risk-based inspection targeting — both narratives are useful for Clarica's messaging. Additionally: in the 2023/24 report, overall ratings showed 4% Outstanding, 78% Good, 16% RI, and 1% Inadequate across all services. The shift to 45% RI/Inadequate among newly inspected homes signals a systemic concern about care quality that AI-assisted compliance monitoring could address.
